rules.summary: Unless otherwise stated, this Part applies to every firm that is
In this Part, the following definitions shall apply:
In this Part, PRA approved person also includes a person in relation to whom a notice under section 59ZZA has been given to an authorised person.

sections.chapter_title: Application to Perform a PRA Senior Management Function

rules.summary: The PRA directs that a firm must make a PRA senior management approval application in accordance with [2.2](/pra-rules/senior-managers-regime---applications-and-notifications#d851aec79fa348558f0
The PRA directs that a firm must use Form A (long form) for a PRA senior management approval application unless:
The PRA directs that, subject to 2.4 and [2.5](/pra-rules/senior-managers-regime---applic
The PRA directs that a firm must not use Form E for a PRA senior management approval application if:
The PRA directs that a firm must not use form E if the person to whom the PRA senior management approval application relates:
1. (1) The PRA directs that a firm must use Form A (shortened form) for a PRA senior management approval application if:
1. (1) The PRA directs that the form submitted for a PRA senior management approval application must be accompanied by a statement of responsibilities in accordance with [Allocation of Responsib
The PRA directs that the form submitted for a PRA senior management application by a designated investment firm must be accompanied by the designated additional information.

sections.chapter_title: Application to Vary a Conditional Approval

rules.summary: The PRA directs that a firm making an application to the PRA under section 63ZA of FSMA (for the variation of a conditional approval) must do so by submitting:

sections.chapter_title: Withdrawal of a Senior Management Approval Application or an Application to Vary a Conditional Approval

rules.summary: The PRA directs that a firm withdrawing an outstanding PRA senior management approval application must do so using Form B.
The PRA directs that a firm withdrawing an application made under section 63ZA of FSMA (Variation of senior managers approval at request of relevant authorised person) must do so using Form B.

sections.chapter_title: Ceasing to Perform a PRA Senior Management Function

rules.summary: 1. (1) A firm must notify the PRA no later than ten business days after a person permanently ceases to perform a PRA senior management function , using:
1. (1) A firm must notify the PRA as soon as practicable after it becomes aware, or has information which reasonably suggests, that it will submit a qualified Form C in respect of a _PRA approved
The PRA directs that if a PRA approved person ceases to perform a PRA senior management function for a firm but continues to perform one or more PRA senior management function for the same _

sections.chapter_title: Change in Details or Responsibilities Relating to PRA Approved Persons

rules.summary: If a PRA approved person's title, name or national insurance number changes, the firm for which the person performs a PRA senior management function must notify the PRA of that change within
If a PRA approved person has been temporarily absent for more than a 12 week period, the firm for which the person performs a PRA senior management function must notify the PRA of:
If, in relation to a firm which has submitted an application on either Form A or Form E, as required by [2.2](/pra-rules/senior-managers-regime---applications-and-notifications#d851aec79fa348558f041
1. (1) The PRA directs that if a firm is required to submit a revised statement of responsibilities under section 62A of FSMA , it must do so by submitting Form J with the revised _statement o
